News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Coast Guard offloads over $49.3 million in illicit drugs interdicted in Eastern Pacific Ocean US Coast Guard 19 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Forward's crew offloaded approximately 6,570 pounds of cocaine worth more than $49.3 million at Port Everglades, Thursday.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Transcript: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Air Force Gen. Dan Caine Hold a Press Briefing DoD 19 Mar 2026
  • Hegseth Draws Distinction Between Epic Fury, Previous Conflicts DoD News 19 Mar 2026 -- During a Pentagon briefing today on Operation Epic Fury, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth said the current conflict with Iran differs from the long wars in Iraq and Afghanistan.
  • Pentagon seeks over 200 bln USD to fund war on Iran: report People's Daily 19 Mar 2026 -- The Pentagon has asked the White House to approve a request exceeding 200 billion U.S. dollars for Congress to fund the ongoing U.S. strikes on Iran, a move likely to stage a major partisan battle on Capitol Hill, The Washington Post reported on Wednesday, citing a senior U.S. official and three other people familiar with the matter.
  • Pentagon Seeks $200B+ for Iran War as Costs Surge — Reports Sputnik 19 Mar 2026 -- The Pentagon has asked the White House to approve a funding request exceeding $200 billion to support the war in Iran, according to The Washington Post, a figure that could face strong resistance in Congress.

  • Israel Widens Scope Of Attacks, Hits Northern Iran, While Energy Sites Burn In Gulf Region RFE/RL 19 Mar 2026 -- Israel widened the scope of its attacks on Iran late on March 18, saying it launched air strikes in the north of the country, with Israeli media saying Iranian naval vessels were being hit on the Caspian Sea coast.

  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 19 Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- On Thursday, March 19, 2026, the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) and the Iranian Army conducted multiple operations as part of Operation True Promise 4, which was launched immediately after the US-Israeli coalition carried out an unprovoked act of aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran on February 28.
  • Drones hit 2 oil refineries in Kuwait, triggering fires Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 19 Mar 2026 -- Kuwait Petroleum Corporation said Thursday that two separate drone attacks targeted operational units at key oil refineries in the country, causing limited fires but no reported injuries, according to Anadolu Agency.
  • Trump should know we have surprises for him, Israel: Commander says after IRGC hits F-35 warplane Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- A senior Iranian military commander says the Islamic Republic has surprises in store for US President Donald Trump, who keeps issuing rhetorical threats against the country, and the Israeli regime.
  • IRGC debuts multi-warhead 'Nasrallah' missile to strike Israel's oil refineries Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has launched the 65th wave of its ongoing retaliatory Operation True Promise 4 targeting Israel's oil refineries, debuting multi-warhead "Nasrallah" ballistic missile.
  • First strike on US F-35: Iran hits stealth jet in central airspace Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says it has successfully hit a US Air Force F-35 stealth fighter jet in central Iran's airspace.
  • Op True Promise, Wave 64: Ben Gurion, Haifa, US Fifth Fleet targeted in retaliatory strikes Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- Iran has launched Wave 64 of Operation True Promise, targeting key military sites in the Israeli occupied territories, including Ben Gurion Airport, fuel supply lines, and strategic military bases in Haifa and Rishon LeZion.
  • IRGC shoots down two hostile drones, hits 80 Israeli targets in new barrage Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has announced that it has shot down two advanced Israeli drones in the skies over the capital Tehran, and the neighboring province of Alborz, while also targeting 80 Israeli sites in a fresh wave of its retaliatory operation.
  • Missile Strikes Hit Ras Laffan Energy Hub in Qatar — MFA Condemns Sputnik 19 Mar 2026 -- QatarEnergy said Ras Laffan Industrial City was targeted by missile attacks, with fires breaking out and "extensive damage" reported.
  • IRGC says ready to defend Iran's maritime borders, counter enemy moves in Strait of Hormuz Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy has expressed its readiness to counter any plots hatched by Iran's enemies in its territorial waters, including the Strait of Hormuz.

  • Trump forced to issue false denial of US role in Israeli attack on Iran's South Pars Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has said he did not know in advance about Israel's terrorist attack on Iran's South Pars gas field, but according to media reports, the attack was coordinated with the United States.
  • Justice Department Disrupts Iranian Cyber Enabled Psychological Operations US Dept. of Justice 19 Mar 2026 -- A Court-Authorized Domain Seizure Removes Four Websites Facilitating the Islamic Republic of Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security's Hacking Efforts Tied to Psychological Operations and Transnational Repression
  • US says may 'unsanction' Iran as IRGC regulates Hormuz traffic Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent says the United States may remove sanctions on Iranian oil stranded in tankers in a bid to increase global supplies and reduce prices in world crude markets.
  • Trump denied he knew Israel was to attack Iran's gas field after Iranian retaliation: Axios Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump knew about Israel's plan to strike Iran's South Pars Gas Field but changed course and denied his prior knowledge after the Islamic Republic hit Qatar's gas field in retaliation, according to an Axios reporter.

  • PM Netanyahu Statement to the Foreign Press Israel - Prime Minister 19 Mar 2026 -- "Our goals are three. One, removing the nuclear threat. Second, removing the ballistic missile threat and removing both of these threats before they're buried deep underground and become immune from aerial attack. And third, this means creating the conditions for the Iranian people to grasp their freedom, to control their destiny."
  • FM Sa'ar speaks India's Minister of External Affairs, Dr. Subrahmanyam Jaishankar Israel MFA 19 Mar 2026 -- FM Sa'ar: "The [Iranian] regime is carrying out naval terrorism in the Strait of Hormuz. This is not an American or Israeli issue, it's a fundamental problem affecting world order and the global economy."
  • Israel's Strike On South Pars Hits Iran, And Iranians, Where It Hurts Most RFE/RL 19 Mar 2026 -- When Israeli jets struck the South Pars gas complex near Asaluyeh, they hit more than pipes and compressors. They struck the single piece of infrastructure most essential to Iran's ability to function -- a field that provides 75 percent of Iran's domestic gas supply and powers roughly 80 percent of the country's electricity generation.

  • Iran's top military command warns of Israel's 'evil intent' to hit regional energy infrastructures Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- Iran's highest operational command unit has warned about an "evil intent" borne by the Israeli regime to target regional energy infrastructures so it can falsely implicate the Islamic Republic and sow regional divisions.
  • Iran warns of no restraint if infrastructure attacks continue Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has issued a stern warning, stating that Iran will exercise no further restraint if its infrastructure faces repeated attacks.
  • IRGC chief lauds martyred Basij commander as 'embodiment of revolutionary spirit' Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- The commander of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) issued a message on Thursday paying tribute to the martyred commander of the Basij forces, hailing him as a product of the Islamic Revolution whose legacy will continue to inspire future generations.
  • Araghchi slams France for failing to utter 'one word of condemnation' of Israel-US war on Iran Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has criticized French President Emmanuel Macron for refraining from uttering even "one word of condemnation" of the military aggression of the United States and the Israeli regime against Iran.
  • Iran protests to UN over US strikes from Persian Gulf states Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- Iran's ambassador and permanent representative to the United Nations has sent letters to the world body and the UN Security Council (UNSC) regarding US assaults on the country from several Persian Gulf countries.
  • Iran urges American citizens to 'disavow this illegal war' Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- Iran says each and every American citizen and official who has a "clear conscience" must "disavow" the ongoing US-Israel military aggression against the Islamic Republic, which started late last month.
  • FM: Iran will never compromise on its people's security Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi emphasizes that Iran will under no circumstances compromise the security of its people.

  • Nearly 100 Israeli-recruited mercenaries captured across Iran Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Intelligence Ministry says its forces have identified and arrested 97 Israel-recruited mercenaries across the Islamic Republic, yet another crushing blow to Israeli-linked spies and traitors who had attempted to take advantage of the war conditions in the country.

  • Middle East war: 'Force of the law' must prevail over the law of force, urges Guterres UN News 19 Mar 2026 -- The US and Israel must end their bombing campaign before it spirals out of control, while Iran needs to stop attacking Gulf neighbours who have played no direct part in the war roiling the Middle East and beyond, the UN Secretary-General urged on Thursday.
  • Middle East war shockwaves ripple through Asia-Pacific fuel and supply chains UN News 19 Mar 2026 -- The fallout from the war in the Middle East is rippling far beyond the Gulf, disrupting fuel supplies, shipping routes and supply chains across Asia and the Pacific, with some of the region's most vulnerable economies already feeling the strain through rising prices, rationing and threats to jobs, food security and remittances.
  • Civilians bear brunt of reckless war in the Middle East, says Türk OHCHR 19 Mar 2026 -- Almost three weeks since the US-Israel strikes on Iran commenced, the conflict continues to spread, with its toll disproportionately impacting civilians across the Middle East region and beyond, UN Human Rights Chief Volker Türk said on Thursday. Attacks now appear to increasingly shift towards densely populated areas, as well as major gas and oil facilities, he warned.

  • China: Assassination of Iranian leaders, attacks on civilian targets unacceptable Press TV 19 Mar 2026 -- A spokesman for China's Foreign Ministry has censured the assassination of Iranian authorities and attacks on civilian targets by the United States and the Israeli regime.
  • Chinese FM says prolonged wars have no winners Global Times 19 Mar 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said on Thursday that there are no winners in the prolonged wars, and a ceasefire and an end to the hostilities are the will of the people.

  • Russia Urges Parties to Iran Conflict to Avoid Attacks on Bushehr Nuclear Plant - Rosatom Sputnik 19 Mar 2026 -- Russia is urging all parties to the Iran conflict to prevent further strikes on the Bushehr nuclear power plant, Russian state nuclear corporation Rosatom CEO Alexey Likhachev said on Thursday.

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Two Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 19 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Two Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 19 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Three Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 19 Mar 2026

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Two Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 19 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Six Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Riyadh and Eastern Regions SPA 19 Mar 2026
  • Minister of Foreign Affairs Holds Meeting with the Foreign Ministers of Egypt, Türkiye and Pakistan SPA 19 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah held a coordination meeting today in Riyadh with the foreign ministers of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, the Republic of Türkiye, and the Arab Republic of Egypt, on the sidelines of the consultative meeting of the foreign ministers of a group of Arab and Islamic countries
  • Minister of Foreign Affairs Participates in Ministerial Consultative Meeting on Iranian Attacks SPA 19 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah participated Wednesday evening in Riyadh in the ministerial consultative meeting attended by foreign ministers and representatives from the Republic of Azerbaijan, the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the United Arab Emirates, the Kingdom of Bahrain, the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, the Republic of Türkiye, the Syrian Arab Republic, the State of Qatar, the State of Kuwait, the Republic of Lebanon and the Arab Republic of Egypt
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Four Drones Intercepted, Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 19 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Three Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 19 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Five Drones Intercepted, Destroyed in Eastern Region Over the Past Hours SPA 19 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Ballistic Missile Launched toward Eastern Region Intercepted, Destroyed SPA 19 Mar 2026

  • Joint statement from the leaders of the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Japan and Canada on the Strait of Hormuz: 19 March 2026 Gov.UK 19 Mar 2026 -- Joint statement from the leaders of the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Japan and Canada on the Strait of Hormuz.
  • European Council conclusions on Middle East EC/CEU 19 Mar 2026
  • PM call with NATO Secretary General Rutte and President Macron of France: 19 March 2026 Gov.UK 19 Mar 2026
  • Australian National Statement in the IMO Council's 36th Extraordinary Session Australia DFAT 19 Mar 2026
  • HE GCCSG: Iran's Dangerous Targeting of SAMREF Refinery, Yanbu Port, and Refinery in South Riyadh in Saudi is Terrorist Act & Blatant Criminal Aggression, Embodying Hostile Nature of Iranian Regime & Its Disregard for Global Stability Gulf Cooperation Council 19 Mar 2026
  • HE GCCSG Condemns Treacherous Iranian Targeting of Mina Abdullah and Mina Al Ahmadi Refineries in Kuwait; Affirms Attacks on Oil Facilities Represent Dangerous Escalation that Undermines Regional Security and Stability Gulf Cooperation Council 19 Mar 2026
  • European Nations, Japan Say Ready To Help Secure Passage Through Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 19 Mar 2026 -- US European allies and Japan express readiness for "appropriate" efforts to secure passage through the key Strait of Hormuz, as the already widespread conflict in the Middle East threatens to spin out of control, although three countries emphasized any effort would only occur after a cease-fire.
  • HE GCCSG: We Condemn in the Strongest Terms Iran's Dangerous Targeting of the Habshan Gas Facility and Bab Field in the UAE, Representing a Grave Aggressive Escalation Gulf Cooperation Council 19 Mar 2026
  • HE GCCSG Discusses Treacherous Iranian Aggressions Against Council States with EU Special Representative for the Gulf Region Gulf Cooperation Council 19 Mar 2026
  • Prime Minister Speaks with Amir of Qatar India PIB 19 Mar 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held a conversation with His Highness Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, the Amir of Qatar, to exchange warm Eid greetings and discuss regional security concerns
  • Prime Minister Speaks with King of Jordan India PIB 19 Mar 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held a telephonic conversation with His Majesty King Abdullah II, the King of Jordan, to exchange festive greetings and discuss the evolving security situation in the region
  • Prime Minister Speaks with President of France India PIB 19 Mar 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held a conversation with President Emmanuel Macron of France to discuss the situation in West Asia and urgent need for de-escalation and return to dialogue and diplomacy
  • Prime Minister Speaks with Prime Minister of Malaysia India PIB 19 Mar 2026 -- The Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held a conversation with Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim of Malaysia to exchange greetings and address regional concerns in West Asia
  • Prime Minister Speaks with Sultan of Oman India PIB 19 Mar 2026 -- The Prime Minister held a productive conversation with Sultan Haitham bin Tariq of Oman to exchange festive greetings and discuss the evolving situation in the region
  • Visit of Deputy Prime Minister/Foreign Minister to Saudi Arabia to Attend Ministerial Meeting of Arab and Islamic Countries Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 19 Mar 2026 -- Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister, Senator Mohammad Ishaq Dar, attended the meeting of the Foreign Ministers of Arab-Islamic countries in Riyadh on 18 March 2026 to discuss recent regional developments.

  • Zineb Riboua: What The War On Iran Means For China And Russia RFE/RL 19 Mar 2026 -- Beijing and Moscow are both close partners of Tehran. With the US-Israeli strikes approaching a third week and Iran retaliating across the region, the conflict is poised to possibly -- and significantly -- affect those ties, the broader roles Russia and China play in the Middle East, and their relations with Washington.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Department Leader Says Nuclear Triad Must Be Upgraded to Meet Dual Threat DoD News 19 Mar 2026 -- With soon to be two near-peer nuclear-armed competitors to deter, the U.S. must upgrade its nuclear forces, which serve as a backdrop to conventional capabilities that must also be robust, War Department leaders told lawmakers March 17.
  • Maritime Theater Missile Defense Forum Advances Interoperability, Relevance at Critical Time US Navy 19 Mar 2026 -- Effective integrated air and missile defense (IAMD) requires an unparalleled level of international cooperation and interoperability, concepts on full display in current operations. The system of systems necessary to be effective, however, requires much more than just collaborative coalition operations.
  • Distributed operations enable Seventh Air Force to sustain airpower during Freedom Shield 26 US Air Force 19 Mar 2026 -- During exercise Freedom Shield 26, the Seventh Air Force tested its ability to command and control airpower in the event its primary Air Operations Center is no longer available, forcing personnel and capabilities to operate from multiple dispersed locations.

  • NSPA Represented at RUSI Combat Air Power Conference NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA) 19 Mar 2026 -- On 12 March, Mr Jas Hawker, the NATO Flight Training Europe (NFTE) Branch Chief, attended the Royal United Services Institute (RUSI) Combat Air Power Conference in London.
  • NATO forces strengthen mine countermeasures in Exercise MCM Baltic NATO Allied Maritime Command 19 Mar 2026 -- NATO ships took part in a major mine countermeasures (MCM) exercise in the Baltic Sea 9-20 March 26. The high-intensity two-week exercise MCM Baltic was led by the German Navy, involving specialist minehunters from nine Allied and Partner nations including those of Standing NATO Mine Countermeasures Group One (SNMCMG1).
  • NATO Secretary General welcomes Romanian President to Headquarters NATO 19 Mar 2026 -- NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte welcomed Romanian President Nicuşor Dan to NATO Headquarters in Brussels today (19 March 2026) for his first official visit, praising Romania's steadfast role as an Ally since joining in 2004.
  • NATO Secretary General commends Bulgaria for its contributions to the Alliance NATO 19 Mar 2026 -- On Thursday (19 March 2026), NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte welcomed the Prime Minister of Bulgaria Andrey Gurov to NATO Headquarters.
  • NATO Allies and Gulf partners discuss the security situation in the Middle East NATO 19 Mar 2026 -- On Wednesday (18 March 2026), the North Atlantic Council met with NATO's partners from the Gulf. Discussions centred on the current security situation in the Middle East and its impact on the region and Euro-Atlantic security.

  • Comment by Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova regarding the remarks of US Secretary of War P.Hegseth on US "space dominance" Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 19 Mar 2026

  • Leeuwin in the lead Australia DOD 19 Mar 2026 -- HMAS Leeuwin is taking on a new role, leading six Pacific nations' Guardian-class patrol boats as a task unit while they transit the 1300 nautical miles to Jervis Bay as part of Exercise Kakadu.
  • Gold Coast Top Guns impress under pressure Australia DOD 19 Mar 2026 -- From Australia's iconic beach to the unforgiving skies of the Nevada desert in the United States, three aviators from the Gold Coast recently tested their skills and teamwork under pressure alongside fighter aircraft and personnel from the United States and United Kingdom.
  • Press conference - Hobart Prime Minister of Australia 19 Mar 2026
  • Meeting of National Cabinet on fuel security and supply chain resilience in response to Middle East conflict Prime Minister of Australia 19 Mar 2026

  • Partner nation in the High North: Pistorius intensifies cooperation with Norway Germany - Federal Ministry of Defence 19 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Defence Boris Pistorius accompanied Federal Chancellor Friedrich Merz to Norway on 12 and 13 March 2026. In Andøya, the Minister's agenda included meetings with Norway's Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre and Defence Minister Tore O. Sandvik.

  • India must become global hub of drone manufacturing in next few years amid current geopolitical instability: Raksha Mantri at National Defence Industries Conclave 2026 India PIB 19 Mar 2026 -- "India must work in a mission mode to emerge as a global hub of indigenous drone manufacturing in the next few years," said Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh as he stressed on the urgent need to build a drone production ecosystem to ensure strategic autonomy, enhance defence preparedness and make the country Aatmanirbhar in view of the present geopolitical uncertainties

  • We look forward to continuing our close collaboration with Syria, other task force members, and the OPCW to drive towards the eradication of Assad's chemical weapon legacy: UK at the UN UK FCDO 19 Mar 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, and the launch of the Breath of Freedom Task Force.

  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna: fighter aircraft of the Russian Federation violated Estonian airspace Estonia MFA 19 Mar 2026 -- Today, 19 March, the Estonian Ministry of Foreign Affairs summoned the chargé d'affaires ad interim of the Russian Federation in Estonia to express a protest and deliver a note regarding the violation of Estonian airspace on 18 March.
  • Lockheed Martin to establish HIMARS sustainment centre in Estonia Estonia MoD 19 Mar 2026 -- At a meeting between Minister of Defence Hanno Pevkur and Lockheed Martin Vice President Paula J. Hartley in Washington, it was agreed that the American defence giant would construct a sustainment centre for HIMARS (High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems) in Estonia, to serve all of the Baltic nations.

  • Decree on certain documents related to export control of dual-use items approved Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Finland 19 Mar 2026 -- The decree on certain documents related to export control of dual-use items has been approved and will enter into force on 15 April 2026. The decree lays down provisions supplementing the Act on the Export Control of Dual-Use Items (500/2024).

  • Baltic Military Conference kicks off in Vilnius Lithuania MND 19 Mar 2026 -- Today, the international Baltic Military Conference begins in Vilnius. The conference addresses key military strategic issues—from strengthening NATO deterrence and defense to enhancing the capabilities of allies and partners, with the aim of effectively responding to evolving threats and ensuring NATO's collective defense.
  • Vilnius will host the 6th Baltic Defence Conference pulling together senior decision, defence policy makers and experts Lithuania MND 19 Mar 2026 -- The Baltic Military Conference kicks off in Vilnius today to address key military strategic issues: from NATO deterrence and defence to enhancement of Allied and partner capabilities aiming to ensure effective response to arising threats and the collective NATO defence.
  • The President at the Baltic Military Conference: Lithuania has chosen to lead by example President of the Republic of Lithuania 19 Mar 2026 -- President Gitanas Nausėda welcomed participants of the Baltic Military Conference taking place in Vilnius on Thursday.

  • SEA SHIELD 2026 - Multinational and Inter-institutional Exercise conducted at the Black Sea Romania - Ministry of National Defence 19 Mar 2026 -- The Romanian Naval Forces will organize, between March 23 and April 3, in the area of responsibility, at sea and on the river, Multinational Exercise SEA SHIELD 2026, the most complex training event of this year.

  • United Arab Emirates - Fixed Site-Low, Slow, Small Unmanned Aircraft Integrated Defeat System US Dept. of State 19 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of the United Arab Emirates to buy Fixed Site- Low, Slow, Small Unmanned Aircraft Integrated Defeat System (FS-LIDS) and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $2.10 billion.
  • United Arab Emirates - Long-Range Discrimination Radar with Terminal High Altitude Area Defense Integration US Dept. of State 19 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) of Long-Range Discrimination Radar with Terminal High Altitude Area Defense Integration and related equipment for an estimated cost of $4.5 billion. The State Department delivered the required certification notifying Congress of this possible sale today.
  • United Arab Emirates - F-16 Munitions and Upgrades US Dept. of State 19 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) F-16 munitions and upgrades. The estimated total cost is $644 million.
  • United Arab Emirates - Advanced Medium-Range Air-to-Air Missiles (AMRAAMs) US Dept. of State 19 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) of Advanced Medium-Range Air-to-Air Missiles (AMRAAMs) and related equipment for an estimated cost of $1.22 billion.

  • Kuwait - Lower Tier Air and Missile Defense Sensor Radars US Dept. of State 19 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Kuwait of Lower Tier Air and Missile Defense Sensor Radars for an estimated cost of $8 billion.

  • Government of Jordan - Aircraft Repair, Return, and Spares US Dept. of State 19 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Jordan of aircraft and munitions support and related equipment for an estimated cost of $70.5 million.

  • PH Army chief says units, soldiers up-to-date with modern tech Philippine News Agency 19 Mar 2026 -- The Pentagon has asked the White House to approve a request exceeding 200 billion U.S. dollars for Congress to fund the ongoing U.S. strikes on Iran, a move likely to stage a major partisan battle on Capitol Hill, The Washington Post reported on Wednesday, citing a senior U.S. official and three other people familiar with the matter.
  • PBBM to PH Army: Enhance your defense capabilities, peace efforts Philippine News Agency 19 Mar 2026 -- President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. on Thursday called on the Philippine Army (PA) to continue strengthening its defense capabilities and advancing peace-building initiatives.
  • PH Army concludes 3-week arms exercise Philippine News Agency 19 Mar 2026 -- The Philippine Army (PA) wrapped up the third edition of its large-scale unilateral Combined Arms Training Exercise "Katihan" on Wednesday.

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Uzbekistan Special Representative for Foreign Affairs Kamilov US Dept. of State 19 Mar 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Guatemalan Foreign Minister Martínez and Minister of Defense General Saenz US Dept. of State 19 Mar 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 19 March 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 19 Mar 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Amid deepening crisis in Palestine, girls face rising risks and a mounting mental health emergency UN News 19 Mar 2026 -- More than two years of unrelenting violence, displacement and loss have pushed children and young people in Palestine into what one UN official describes as a "profound mental health emergency", with girls facing heightened risks, including a resurgence in child marriage.
  • As aid restrictions deepen crisis in the occupied Palestinian territories, women and girls pay heightened price, warn UN experts OHCHR 19 Mar 2026 -- Israel's restrictions on humanitarian operations in Gaza and the West Bank are inflicting severe and escalating gender-specific harm, UN experts* warned today.
  • State-backed terror squads at the forefront of Israel's ethnic cleansing and annexation policy in the West Bank, warn UN experts OHCHR 19 Mar 2026 -- UN experts* today condemned Israel's accelerating campaign of ethnic cleansing and annexation in the occupied West Bank, including East Jerusalem, marked by the forced displacement of Palestinians and escalating violence by Israeli security forces and settlers terrorising Palestinian communities.

  • Comment by Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova on Israeli attack on RT television channel journalists, March 19, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 19 Mar 2026
  • Spain condemns attacks on critical energy infrastructure in Middle East and calls for immediate cessation Spain MFA 19 Mar 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Ukraine, Sudan UNOCHA 19 Mar 2026

  • DND rejects China's 'farce' tag on PH ops in Bajo de Masinloc Philippine News Agency 19 Mar 2026 -- The Department of National Defense (DND) on Thursday rejected China's claim that legitimate Philippine maritime operations near Bajo de Masinloc (also known as Scarborough Shoal) are a "self-directed farce."

  • 187,000 in South Sudan Risk Losing Life-Saving Services as USD 6 Million Funding Gap Looms IOM 19 Mar 2026 -- Life-saving services for nearly 187,000 displaced people in South Sudan could collapse within weeks due to a USD 6 million funding shortfall, the International Organization for Migration (IOM) warned today.

  • Colombia: Urgent action needed to end widespread violence against human rights defenders - UN report OHCHR 19 Mar 2026 -- Human rights defenders in Colombia have been subjected to unrelenting violence over the past decade, with on average just under 100 killed every year, a report issued by the UN Human Rights Office finds, calling for urgent measures by the authorities to address policy and structural issues contributing to this toll.

  • OPAPRU to MILF: Continue working with gov't on CAB implementation Philippine News Agency 19 Mar 2026 -- The Office of the Presidential Adviser on Peace, Reconciliation and Unity (OPAPRU) called on the Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF) to continue engaging with the government on the implementation of the Comprehensive Agreement on the Bangsamoro (CAB).

News Reports

  • Three Charged with Conspiring to Unlawfully Divert Cutting Edge U.S. Artificial Intelligence Technology to China US Dept. of Justice 19 Mar 2026 -- Today, an indictment was unsealed charging Yih-Shyan "Wally" Liaw, Ruei-Tsang "Steven" Chang, and Ting-Wei "Willy" Sun, for allegedly conspiring to divert high-performance computer servers assembled in the United States and integrating sophisticated U.S. artificial intelligence technology to China, in violation of U.S. export controls laws. Liaw, a U.S. citizen, and Sun, a citizen of Taiwan, were arrested today and will be presented in the Northern District of California. Chang, a citizen of Taiwan, remains a fugitive.

  • Expanded meeting of the Prosecutor General Office's Board President of Russia 19 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin spoke at the expanded meeting of the Russian Prosecutor General Office's Board.
  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna: the European Commission has been given a clear mandate to seek solutions to prevent Russian fighters from entering Europe Estonia MFA 19 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna said that the inclusion of the issue of Russian combatants in the conclusions of the European Council held today, 19 March, shows that Estonia has brought an entirely new topic to the forefront of attention in Europe and has, in a short period of time, managed to secure support for it among EU member states.
  • A Kremlin Cheerleader Rips Into Putin. A Day Later, He's In A Psychiatric Hospital. RFE/RL 19 Mar 2026 -- In the months before and after Aleksei Navalny's January 2021 return to Russia, a bombastic pro-Kremlin lawyer named Ilya Remeslo led the public charge against the gadfly opposition activist and his Anti-Corruption Foundation.
  • Out Of Office: A New Twist In Kremlin Efforts To Conceal Putin's Location RFE/RL 19 Mar 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin is getting harder to find. In November, Systema, RFE/RL's Russian investigative unit, revealed that the Kremlin was obscuring Putin's location and keeping much of his travel under wraps by using three nearly identical offices in different parts of Russia for meetings.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on March 19, 2026 PRC MofA 19 Mar 2026
  • Wang Yi Has a Phone Call with UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper PRC MofA 19 Mar 2026

  • US intelligence report shows shifted tone on Taiwan question; 'China threat' narrative persists despite signs of 'softened stance' Global Times 19 Mar 2026 -- The US intelligence community released its 2026 annual threat assessment report on Wednesday, which though viewed by some US media outlets as having "hints of a softened US stance," still hypes "China threat" rhetoric, over which a Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson on Thursday urged relevant US agencies and personnel to discard ideological bias and Cold-War and zero-sum mentality.
  • FM responds to US report claiming China has no plan to resolve Taiwan question in 2027 Global Times 19 Mar 2026 -- Commenting on US intelligence agencies' annual threat assessment report, which claimed that China does not currently plan to resolve the Taiwan question in 2027 and seeks to "achieve unification without the use of force," Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Thursday that the Taiwan question is China's internal affair, and how to resolve the question is entirely a matter for the Chinese people and allows no interference from any external forces. The US should adhere to the one-China principle and the three China-US joint communiqués, and exercise caution in words and deeds on the Taiwan question.
  • FM responds to Japan's rejection of US claim of its shift on Taiwan Global Times 19 Mar 2026
  • FM responds to DPP's move over ROK's 'China Taiwan' label on entry documents Global Times 19 Mar 2026 -- When asked to comment on reports that the ROK listed Taiwan as "China (Taiwan)" on its electronic arrival card system, and the Taiwan authorities have claimed that they will change the designation of "Korea" to "South Korea" on some of its entry and exit documents, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Thursday that Taiwan is an inalienable part of China's territory. The one-China principle is a basic norm in international relations and a prevailing international consensus.
  • China not planning to invade Taiwan in 2027: U.S. intelligence report CNA 19 Mar 2026 -- China currently does not plan to invade Taiwan in 2027, but it continues to develop military capabilities and contingency plans to use force if necessary, according to an annual report released Wednesday by the United States Intelligence Community.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 19 Mar 2026

  • Spokesperson's Press Briefing (March 19, 2026) ROK MFA 19 Mar 2026

  • European Council: Doorstep remarks by High Representative Kaja Kallas European External Action Service (EEAS) 19 Mar 2026
  • Statement by President von der Leyen at the joint press conference with President Costa following the meeting of the European Council of March 2026 European Commission 19 Mar 2026

  • PM call with His Highness the Crown Prince of Kuwait: 19 March 2026 Gov.UK 19 Mar 2026
  • PM call with the Amir of Qatar: 19 March 2026 Gov.UK 19 Mar 2026
  • PM meeting with President Bola Ahmed Tinubu of Nigeria: 19 March 2026 Gov.UK 19 Mar 2026

  • Press conference of the Federal Chancellor after the European Council - Strength and sovereignty for Europe Germany - Federal Government 19 Mar 2026 -- Press conference of the Federal Chancellor after the European Council

  • Minister Anand meets with United Kingdom's Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs Canada 19 Mar 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, yesterday met the Honourable Yvette Cooper, Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, in London, United Kingdom, during a 2-day visit.

  • Washington Eases Sanctions On Minsk As Belarus Releases 250 Prisoners RFE/RL 19 Mar 2026 -- The United States has lifted sanctions on several Belarusian entities and the country's Finance Ministry as Minsk announced the release of scores of prisoners -- including political detainees Kim Samusenka and journalist Katsyaryna Andreyeva.

  • Russia in Dialogue With Cuban Leadership, Discussing Options for Assistance - Kremlin Sputnik 19 Mar 2026 -- Russia is in dialogue with the leadership of Cuba and is discussing options for providing assistance, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Thursday.

  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: EXPANSION OF NUCLEAR POWER GENERATION CAPACITY India PIB 19 Mar 2026 -- The present installed nuclear power capacity in the country is 8,780 MW comprising of 24 nuclear power plants (excluding RAPS-1 - 100 MW)
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: NUCLEAR ISSUES India PIB 19 Mar 2026
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: NUCLEAR ENERGY GENERATION India PIB 19 Mar 2026

  • "Türkiye is step by step advancing towards its goals" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 19 Mar 2026 -- Issuing a message on Eid al-Fitr, President Erdoğan said: "Türkiye, with Allah's permission, is secure, in safe hands, and step by step advancing towards its goals under the management of experienced and competent cadres. As the government and the alliance, we will work round-the-clock if need be, we will work 365 days and 6 hours if need be, but we will not allow anyone to stand in Türkiye's way."

  • SpaceX fires up next-gen 'V3' Starship for 1st time ahead of April launch Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 19 Mar 2026 -- SpaceX's biggest, most powerful Starship to date just breathed fire for the first time, according to Space.com.

  • Anutin Charnvirakul re-elected Thailand's PM People's Daily 19 Mar 2026 -- Anutin Charnvirakul, a candidate from the Bhumjaithai Party, was re-elected Thailand's prime minister after securing a simple majority in a vote cast by the House of Representatives on Thursday.

  • World News in Brief: Rafah crossing reopens, gender inequality worsens global water crisis, rights defenders in Colombia UN News 19 Mar 2026 -- The Rafah crossing into Gaza reopened on Thursday for limited movement in both directions after a 20-day suspension, according to the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA).
